explicit_text: I grew up in North Asheville, specifically in the lake section called Beaver Lake. It was a close-knit community where all the children played outside, climbing trees and hiking around Reynolds Mountain. My family and neighbors were quite similar in lifestyle, and it was an open childhood filled with outdoor activities.

explicit_text: Beaver Lake was where my family first settled in North Asheville. My father’s parents built one of the first houses on the left side of the lake when they moved here from Alabama in 1926 for my grandfather’s health. Later, my family built another house on the right side near Kingwood Place and Wembley, where I grew up for eight years.

explicit_text: My family originally came from Alabama, where my grandmother, Hattie Hibernia Hooker, was a pioneering woman legislator and schoolteacher.
